re_line: KOSPI crashes 9% on Middle East tensions · Yoon sentenced to 2 years · SMR trilateral deal · DPRK nuclear expansion continues
morning_memo: Seoul shares plunged 9 percent on Monday — triggering a circuit breaker — as tech losses compounded Middle East tension fears, with the KRX bourse operator activating both a sell-side sidecar and a full circuit breaker.
A Seoul district court sentenced former President Yoon Suk-yeol to two years in prison in the 'free opinion poll' case, separate from the ongoing martial law proceedings.

South Korea, the United States, and Japan formalized trilateral cooperation on small modular reactors amid surging power demand from chip fabs and AI data centers, with the agreement framed against geopolitical rivalry in the clean energy sector.

key_stat.label: ROK export growth, first 10 days of July (year-on-year)
key_stat.context: Chip shipments drove the surge in the first 10-day reporting period of July, even as the KOSPI crashed 9% on the same day on tech-driven selling.
key_stat.source: Yonhap English — 'Exports up 54 pct in first 10 days of July'

imagery_report.label: DPRK luxury compound renovations
imagery_report.headline: Satellite images reveal renovation of 9 DPRK luxury compounds attributed to Kim Jong Un
imagery_report.body: Satellite imagery cited by WION shows a makeover of nine luxury compounds associated with Kim Jong Un in North Korea. The report does not specify which analytical organization produced the imagery assessment, and the outlet's framing is tabloid in register. No assessment of operational or security significance is provided in the source article. Readers should treat this with caution pending corroboration from established imagery houses (38 North, AEI, Beyond Parallel).

bp_locations.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
bp_locations.note: Apr 2026: Enrichment building expansion complete, new centrifuge hall operational. 5 MWe reactor and IRT-2000 both running. Thermal signatures consistent with increased plutonium/HEU production. AEI's June 2026 special report on DPRK constitutional amendments notes the nuclear program's posture is now formally entrenched in state law.

bp_locations.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
bp_locations.note: Jun 30, 2026: NK Pro satellite imagery shows the Choe Hyon destroyer, commissioned June 25 by Kim Jong Un, remains docked at a west coast civilian cargo port one week after KCNA claimed it departed for operations. NK Pro also uncovered previously unreported details about where a test of its sister ship (Kang Kon) was conducted on the east coast. Feb 2025: Hero Kim Kun Ok SLBM submarine exited drydock after 2+ years of fitting-out but still not operational as of May 2025. New submarine construction begun in separate bay.

bp_locations.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
bp_locations.note: Jun 21, 2026: All six THAAD launchers returned to the permanent Seongju site from Osan Air Base, restoring the battery's full deployment footprint. Battery operational status remains subject to ongoing assessment after the partial Middle East redeployment.

bp_locations.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
bp_locations.note: May 2026: Rocket engine test conducted last week, possibly for military reconnaissance satellite program revival. New viewing area building nearing completion. Previous reports showed 2,500 kN engine test stand firing and facility expansion.

bp_locations.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: ROK DIA assessed Tunnel No. 3 is test-ready and a nuclear test can be conducted at any time upon political decision. Site maintained at high readiness since mid-2024.

bp_locations.name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
bp_locations.note: Jul 1–3, 2026: 38 North assesses the opening of the new Tumen River vehicle bridge as unlikely in the near term; customs infrastructure remains incomplete on both sides despite the bridge nearing physical completion. Earlier reporting had flagged a June 19 scheduled opening. Passenger rail service resumed mid-2025; ore and tank car traffic at highest levels since project began.

bp_locations.name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
bp_locations.note: Mar 12, 2026: Cross-border passenger train service resumed between Sinuiju and Dandong after COVID-era suspension. New Yalu River Bridge may open in 2026. NK Pro satellite imagery analysis published July 7, 2026 shows over a dozen makeshift smuggling routes across the NK-China border that went quiet since late 2025 have appeared active again in recent weeks.

bp_locations.name: Rason SEZ
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: Chinese businesspeople re-entry to Rason SEZ expanded. Coal exports through Rason at highest levels in years throughout 2025. Arms export berth also active.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea NLL
bp_locations.note: Jul 12, 2026: A ROK Navy seaman apprentice went missing during an overnight East Sea patrol approximately 50km off Geojin, Gangwon Province, near the NLL; Seoul notified Pyongyang and deployed ~10 ships and aircraft. The sailor's body was subsequently recovered by the Navy. Sep 26, 2025: 140m DPRK merchant vessel Toksong crossed NLL near Baengnyeong Island, advanced 5km south; ROK Navy fired ~60 warning shots.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea PMZ
bp_locations.note: Atlantic Amsterdam (converted oil rig, Chinese aquaculture platform) relocated out of PMZ Jan 27-28, 2026 to Weihai shipyard following Lee Jae-myung Beijing state visit. Two giant aquaculture cages (Shen Lan 1, Shen Lan 2) and 13 buoys remain in the PMZ.

bp_locations.name: Vostochny/Dunai (Russian Far East)
bp_locations.note: Jan 2026: Arms shipments slowed due to ice — one Russian vessel (likely Angara or Lady R) visited Rason, docked Jan 14 at arms export berth. Cargo routed to Vostochny. Since Sep 2023: 64 voyages by 4 vessels delivered ~15,800 containers from Rajin to Vostochny/Dunai.

rok_government.action: Lee announces 'future response' fund from extra tax revenue
rok_government.detail: President Lee said the government will create a 'future response' fund using surplus tax revenue. The announcement follows the budget ministry's projection that South Korea expects to collect at least 500 trillion won in tax revenue in 2027.

rok_government.action: Lee to meet Britain's Princess Anne, discuss bilateral cooperation
rok_government.detail: President Lee Jae-myung is scheduled to meet Princess Anne, the younger sister of King Charles III, to discuss bilateral cooperation. Princess Anne is visiting South Korea to mark Korean War battle anniversaries and to join an event marking bilateral maritime exchanges.

rok_government.ministry: Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy
rok_government.action: ROK, US, Japan trilateral SMR cooperation agreement formalized
rok_government.detail: South Korea, the United States, and Japan bolstered trilateral cooperation on small modular reactors amid surging power demand driven by chip fabrication facilities and AI data centers, framed against geopolitical competition in the clean energy sector.

rok_government.action: ROK budget minister projects 500+ trillion won tax revenue in 2027
rok_government.detail: South Korea's budget minister stated the government expects to collect at least 500 trillion won in tax revenue in 2027, providing the fiscal basis for President Lee's announced 'future response' fund.

rok_government.ministry: Ministry of the Interior and Safety / Government Task Force
rok_government.action: Government launches task force to address stalking murders
rok_government.detail: A government task force was launched Monday to devise countermeasures against stalking-related murders, reflecting ongoing public concern over violent crime linked to stalking behavior.

rok_government.action: Ruling party and government vow joint support for chip cluster and AI investment
rok_government.detail: The ruling Democratic Party and the government announced joint efforts to support the semiconductor chip cluster and AI investment projects, signaling coordinated industrial policy heading into the 2027 budget cycle.

calendar_watch.headline: Section 122 surcharge expires
calendar_watch.detail: The 10% global Section 122 surcharge hits its 150-day statutory limit on July 24 unless Congress acts to extend it. Without extension or a new legal vehicle, the US loses its broadest tariff authority over South Korean goods — the USTR Section 301 investigation remains the fallback mechanism.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean War armistice 73rd anniversary
calendar_watch.detail: The 73rd anniversary of the July 27, 1953 armistice. DPRK has been building escalatory framing ahead of this date; Kim Jong Un's July 11-12 military command meeting formally converted the June 25 plenum nuclear expansion directives into state policy, with the armistice anniversary now 14 days out.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean Liberation Day (광복절)
calendar_watch.detail: National holiday marking Korea's liberation from Japanese colonial rule in 1945. The ROK president traditionally delivers a politically significant address. Watch for Lee Jae-myung's framing on inter-Korean policy and Japan relations.

calendar_watch.headline: Ulchi Freedom Shield (UFS) — US-ROK combined exercise (est.)
calendar_watch.detail: The annual US-ROK combined Ulchi Freedom Shield exercise is typically held in mid-August for approximately 11 days. DPRK historically escalates rhetorical and military activity in response. Exact dates not yet confirmed in today's sources.

calendar_watch.headline: DPRK founding day — 78th anniversary
calendar_watch.detail: September 9 marks the 78th anniversary of the DPRK's founding. The date historically accompanies military parades and missile tests. With Kim's June 25 plenum ordering expanded nuclear forces and two new warships annually for five years, this anniversary warrants close monitoring.

top_stories.headline: KOSPI crashes 9%, circuit breaker triggered, on tech losses and Middle East tensions
top_stories.body: Seoul shares plunged 9 percent on Monday, prompting KRX to activate both a sell-side sidecar and a full circuit breaker — the most severe market intervention mechanism — as investors sold technology shares amid renewed Middle East uncertainty. The financial watchdog chief urged better consumer protection amid the volatility. South Korea's export surge of 53.9% in the first 10 days of July, driven by chips, provided a stark contrast with the equity selloff.
top_stories.so_what: BOK is widely expected to raise its benchmark rate to curb mounting inflation pressure at its next meeting, adding a monetary tightening decision to an already stressed equity market environment.

top_stories.headline: Ex-President Yoon sentenced to 2 years in prison in 'free opinion poll' case
top_stories.body: A Seoul district court sentenced former President Yoon Suk-yeol to two years in prison in a separate criminal case involving a 'free opinion poll,' distinct from the ongoing martial law-related proceedings. The special counsel team separately announced it will question former first lady Kim Keon-hee over the presidential residence relocation project. A bail request by a former chief presidential aide jailed in connection with the Yongsan relocation probe was also denied the same day.
top_stories.so_what: Multiple simultaneous legal proceedings against Yoon and his former inner circle continue to move through the courts in parallel with the martial law impeachment-related cases.

top_stories.headline: S. Korea, US, Japan formalize trilateral SMR cooperation amid chip-driven power demand surge
top_stories.body: South Korea, the United States, and Japan bolstered trilateral cooperation on small modular reactors, with the agreement framed against surging electricity demand from semiconductor fabrication facilities and AI data centers, as well as geopolitical competition in the clean energy sector. The deal follows a separate Yonhap report that chip fabs and AI data centers are putting South Korea's nuclear energy policy back in focus. South Korea's ruling Democratic Party and government have pledged joint efforts to support semiconductor cluster and AI investment projects.
top_stories.so_what: Directly relevant to the US-Korea $350B investment framework's strategic sectors bucket and to ROK energy policy decisions on nuclear power expansion.

also_today.headline: NK Pro: North Korea demolishing gray markets, replacing with standardized '20×10' facilities
also_today.body_text: NK Pro satellite imagery analysis shows North Korea is tearing down long-standing town markets and replacing them with standardized covered facilities under Kim Jong Un's '20×10' rural development project. The replacement facilities are outdoor covered structures built at new leisure sites — the report provides the first satellite confirmation of this market restructuring program at scale.

also_today.headline: Body of ROK Navy sailor missing during East Sea patrol recovered
also_today.body_text: The ROK Navy recovered the body of a seaman apprentice who went missing during an overnight East Sea patrol approximately 50km off Geojin, Gangwon Province. Seoul had notified Pyongyang of the incident and deployed around 10 ships and aircraft in the search; NK News separately reported Seoul sought North Korea's assistance.

also_today.headline: Hyundai Rotem receives NATO quality assurance certificate for first time
also_today.body_text: The state-run defense quality assurance agency issued a NATO quality assurance certificate to Hyundai Rotem for the first time, a step that could facilitate ROK defense exports to NATO member states. Hyundai Rotem produces the K2 main battle tank, which Poland and other NATO allies have ordered.

also_today.headline: AMCHAM launches 'K-Doorknock' program to support Korean firms' US expansion
also_today.body_text: The American Chamber of Commerce in Korea launched the 'K-Doorknock' program to help Korean companies expand their presence in the United States. The program is designed to facilitate introductions and business development as Korean firms increase US investment under the $350B bilateral investment framework.

overnight_items.headline: N.K. premier Pak Thae-song returns from China, vows expanded ties
overnight_items.body_text: North Korean Premier Pak Thae-song returned from a visit to China — during which he met President Xi Jinping — and vowed efforts to expand ties with Beijing on the 65th anniversary of the China-DPRK friendship treaty. Xi said China's commitment to North Korea friendship will not change. The visit consolidates diplomatic signaling between Beijing and Pyongyang as DPRK's formal nuclear expansion decision — announced at the July 11-12 military command meeting — becomes state policy.

overnight_items.headline: Samsung begins preparations to manufacture Tesla's AI5 chip
overnight_items.body_text: Samsung Electronics has begun preparations to produce Tesla's AI5 chip, adding a high-profile AI semiconductor client to its foundry order book. The announcement comes as Samsung separately moved forward the opening of its first Yongin chip plant to 2029 and as the ruling party and government pledge joint support for the semiconductor cluster, underscoring the centrality of chip manufacturing to both commercial strategy and national industrial policy.

overnight_items.headline: Chip fabs and AI data centers push ROK nuclear energy policy to forefront
overnight_items.body_text: South Korea's energy policy is at a crossroads as semiconductor fabrication facilities and AI data centers drive surging electricity demand, according to Yonhap analysis. The simultaneous formalization of the US-Japan-Korea SMR trilateral agreement reflects both the commercial urgency and the geopolitical dimension of the clean energy buildout. ROK's nuclear energy debate is now directly linked to the competitiveness of its semiconductor industry.

business_economy.headline: HD KSOE wins 545.6 billion-won contract for three ammonia carriers
business_economy.body_text: HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering secured a 545.6 billion-won contract to build three ammonia carriers, continuing the ROK shipbuilding sector's strong order momentum in alternative-fuel vessel types. The contract adds to Korea's dominant share of global green-fuel vessel orders.

business_economy.headline: BOK: AI chip supercycle continuing; dismisses 'peak-out' concerns
business_economy.body_text: The Bank of Korea assessed that the global semiconductor supercycle driven by AI demand is ongoing and dismissed concerns about a demand peak, even as the KOSPI's tech-sector losses triggered a circuit breaker on the same day. The BOK is separately expected to raise its benchmark rate to address inflation at its next meeting.

business_economy.headline: BOK widely expected to raise benchmark rate to curb inflation
business_economy.body_text: The Bank of Korea is widely expected to increase its benchmark interest rate at the next Monetary Policy Board meeting to counter mounting inflation pressure. The current rate stands at 2.50%, last changed in July 2026.

northeast_asia.headline: Britain's Princess Anne to visit ROK, mark Korean War battle anniversaries
northeast_asia.body_text: Princess Anne will visit South Korea to mark Korean War battle anniversaries involving British forces and to join an event commemorating bilateral maritime exchanges, with a presidential meeting with Lee Jae-myung scheduled. The visit falls 14 days before the July 27 armistice anniversary.

gallup_spotlight.finding: 46% of respondents rate the government's real estate policy negatively versus 26% positively; 55% expect home prices to rise (Gallup Korea, Week 1 of July 2026).

top_articles.headline: Seoul seeks North Korea's help after sailor goes missing at sea
top_articles.summary: Following the disappearance and subsequent death of a ROK Navy seaman apprentice during an East Sea patrol, Seoul notified Pyongyang and requested North Korea's assistance in the search — an inter-Korean communications channel use that is rare but not unprecedented.

senior_officials.name: Pak Thae-song
senior_officials.activity: Returned from Beijing after meeting Xi Jinping on the 65th anniversary of the China-DPRK friendship treaty, pledging expanded bilateral exchanges and cooperation.

kcna_delta.bottom_line: KCNA output today is thin — the lead item is Seoul's inter-Korean notification over the missing sailor rather than state propaganda. Watch for KCNA escalatory framing ahead of the July 27 armistice anniversary, 14 days out, as Kim's nuclear expansion directive has now been formalized as state policy.

investment_package.latest_update: AMCHAM Korea launched the 'K-Doorknock' program July 13 to help Korean companies expand US presence; no new WH-verified deal announced today.

known_deals.company: Samsung Heavy Industries / Vigor Marine Group

trade_policy.detail: Launched Mar 11, 2026 against 16 trading partners including South Korea; provides potential legal authority for tariffs post-Section 122 expiry on Jul 24, 2026.

trade_policy.item: BIS Semiconductor Export Controls — Samsung/SK Hynix China fabs
trade_policy.detail: Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs operating under annual BIS export licenses renewed Dec 30, 2025 for 2026; maintenance and incremental upgrades only, no expansion permitted.
Foreign Entity of Concern rules affect Korean EV battery makers (LG Energy Solution, SK On, Samsung SDI) sourcing from China; battery components phase-in Dec 2024, critical minerals Dec 2025.

trade_policy.item: CHIPS Act Guardrails — Samsung Taylor TX
trade_policy.detail: Samsung received preliminary $6.4B CHIPS award for Taylor TX fabs; 10-year restriction on materially expanding semiconductor manufacturing in countries of concern; Samsung Xi'an NAND fab limited to legacy production.

tariff_tracker.headline_note: 10% Section 122 global surcharge applies to South Korean goods; expires Jul 24, 2026 at the 150-day statutory limit unless Congress acts to extend or a new legal vehicle is established.

sector_rates.note: All country exemptions/TRQs eliminated Mar 2025; 50% applies universally with no quota arrangement for Korea.
Reduced from 25% under US-Korea deal; retroactive to Nov 1, 2025 per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.
Reduced under US-Korea deal per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.
Effective Jan 15, 2026; broader semiconductor tariffs pending Commerce/USTR report; deal promises Korea terms no less favorable than comparable partners.
Rate cap agreed in US-Korea deal but specific Section 232 action not yet issued.
Tariffs removed per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.

sector_rates.sector: Generic Pharma / Precursors / Rare Natural Resources

tariff_tracker.section_122_surcharge: 10% global surcharge (Section 122, effective Feb 24, 2026, expires Jul 24, 2026)
tariff_tracker.last_change: Feb 24, 2026 — 10% Section 122 surcharge imposed following Supreme Court's Feb 20, 2026 invalidation of IEEPA tariffs (6-3)
tariff_tracker.next_trigger: Jul 24, 2026 — Section 122 surcharge expiry (150-day statutory limit); Section 301 investigations ongoing against South Korea and 15 other partners
